experience.title: Software Development Intern
bullets: Developed software for a full-stack banking application used by internal teams, with emphasis on efficiency and secure workflows.
Implemented React-based frontend flows and supported backend integration to improve internal banking operations.
Developed backend and BFF layers with Java and Spring Boot, and managed PostgreSQL-based data access.

experience.title: Software Development Intern
bullets: Developed a mobile application for real-time bus tracking and card balance inquiry.

Applied MVVM architecture to support maintainability, debugging, and code efficiency.

projects.name: Unity Puzzle Game Prototype
bullets: Developed a casual puzzle-game prototype in Unity with C#, focusing on interactive gameplay systems.
Designed a grid-based mechanic for object interaction and reusable level structures.
Implemented animation-supported gameplay feedback and logic for responsive player interaction.

projects.name: Procedural Level System for Puzzle Gameplay
bullets: Built systems capable of dynamically generating levels for replayable puzzle experiences.
Designed level logic with a focus on maintainable game architecture and reusable components.
Strengthened object-oriented problem-solving through gameplay-system implementation in Unity.
projects.replaces: A Decision Support System for Route Optimization of Last-Mile and Last-Yard Deliveries

projects.name: Mobile Transportation Tracking Application
bullets: Developed a mobile app for real time bus tracking and card balance inquiry during my internship at Asis Elektronik ve Bilişim Sistemleri A.Ş.
Integrated the TFE Open Data API to provide users with live bus locations and route information.
Followed MVVM architecture to ensure clean, maintainable, and scalable code.

projects.name: Word Puzzle Game Prototype
bullets: Developed a word puzzle game prototype inspired by Words of Wonders-style mechanics.
Implemented letter selection, word validation, and progression-oriented gameplay logic.
Focused on recreating interaction patterns and core gameplay systems through reverse-engineering.

projects.name: Match-Style Game Prototype
bullets: Built a match-style casual game prototype inspired by Candy Crush-like grid interactions.
Implemented tile-matching mechanics, gameplay loops, and state management.
Explored mobile puzzle-game design patterns through rapid prototyping in Unity.
